Package Index | Class Index
Summary: Nested Classes | Methods | Inherited Methods | [Expand All]
public class

FunctionalUtils

extends Object
java.lang.Object
   ↳ com.android.internal.util.FunctionalUtils

Class Overview

Utilities specific to functional programming

Summary

Nested Classes
interface FunctionalUtils.RemoteExceptionIgnoringConsumer<T> A Consumer that automatically ignores any RemoteExceptions. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingBiConsumer<A, B> A BiConsumer that allows throwing checked exceptions from its single abstract method. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingBiFunction<T, U, R> A BiFunction that allows throwing checked exceptions from its single abstract method. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingChecked2Consumer<Input, ExceptionOne extends Exception, ExceptionTwo extends Exception> A Consumer that allows the caller to specify 2 different custom checked Exceptions that can be thrown by the implementer. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingCheckedConsumer<Input, ExceptionType extends Exception> A Consumer that allows the caller to specify a custom checked Exception that can be thrown by the implementer. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingCheckedFunction<Input, Output, ExceptionType extends Exception> A Function that allows the caller to specify a custom checked Exception that can be thrown by the implementer. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingCheckedSupplier<Output, ExceptionType extends Exception> A Supplier that allows the caller to specify a custom checked Exception that can be thrown by the implementer. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingConsumer<T> A Consumer that allows throwing checked exceptions from its single abstract method. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingFunction<T, R> A Function that allows throwing checked exceptions from its single abstract method. 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingRunnable An equivalent of Runnable that allows throwing checked exceptions This can be used to specify a lambda argument without forcing all the checked exceptions to be handled within it 
interface FunctionalUtils.ThrowingSupplier<T> An equivalent of Supplier that allows throwing checked exceptions This can be used to specify a lambda argument without forcing all the checked exceptions to be handled within it 
Public Methods
static String getLambdaName(Object function)
Gets a user-friendly name for a lambda function.
static Runnable handleExceptions(FunctionalUtils.ThrowingRunnable r, Consumer<Throwable> handler)
Wraps the given FunctionalUtils.ThrowingRunnable into one that handles any exceptions using the provided handler
static <T> Consumer<T> ignoreRemoteException(RemoteExceptionIgnoringConsumer<T> action)
Wraps a given action into one that ignores any RemoteExceptions
static Runnable uncheckExceptions(FunctionalUtils.ThrowingRunnable action)
static <T> Consumer<T> uncheckExceptions(ThrowingConsumer<T> action)
Converts a lambda expression that throws a checked exception(s) into a regular Consumer by propagating any checked exceptions as RuntimeException
static <A, B> BiConsumer<A, B> uncheckExceptions(ThrowingBiConsumer<A, B> action)
static <T> Supplier<T> uncheckExceptions(ThrowingSupplier<T> action)
static <I, O> Function<I, O> uncheckExceptions(ThrowingFunction<I, O> action)
[Expand]

handler Consumer

Runnable

public static Consumer<T> uncheckExceptions (ThrowingConsumer<T> action)

Converts a lambda expression that throws a checked exception(s) into a regular Consumer by propagating any checked exceptions as RuntimeException

Parameters
action ThrowingConsumer
Returns
Consumer<T>
